merger_name: Ochre Health - Airlie Women's Clinic, VIC

acquirers.name: Ochre Group Commercial Property Pty Ltd

merger_description:
Ochre Health Pty Limited proposes to acquire all of the shares in the Airlie Women’s Clinic Pty Ltd (**AWC**) through a share sale agreement, and Ochre Group Commercial Property Pty Ltd as trustee for Ochre Group Commercial Property Unit Trust proposes to acquire the land on which AWC’s general practice medical clinics on Malvern Road, Malvern VIC and Airlie Avenue, Prahran VIC are located. The parties are seeking a notification waiver in respect of both the share and land acquisitions (together, the **Acquisition**).

The acquirers (together with their connected entities, **Ochre Health)** support independent doctors to provide general practice medical services in regional, rural and urban communities around Australia.

Ochre Health currently operates 69 medical centres and urgent care clinics located in the ACT, NSW, Queensland, Tasmania and Victoria, but currently does not operate any medical centres or urgent care clinics in Melbourne, Victoria. Ochre Health Group’s medical recruitment business, Ochre Recruitment, also works with hospitals and medical practices across Australia and NZ to source and place locum and permanent doctors across a range of specialties.

Ochre Health is majority owned and controlled by Genesis Capital, an Australian-based private equity firm that invests in businesses operating in the healthcare sector.

The target, AWC, is a family medical practice with an all-female team of doctors providing comprehensive general practice medical care to men, women and children from its 3 clinics in the southeastern Melbourne suburbs of Prahran and Malvern, Victoria. As noted above, as part of the Acquisition, AWC is selling the land on which 2 of its 3 clinics is located.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Ochre Health Pty Limited applied for a notification waiver in respect
of:
• its proposed acquisition of all of the shares in Airlie
Women’s Clinic Pty Ltd (AWC), and
• Ochre Group Commercial Property Pty Ltd as trustee for
Ochre Group Commercial Property Unit Trust’s proposed
acquisition of the land on which 2 of AWC’s 3 general
practice medical clinics are operated,
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirers, Ochre Health Pty Limited and Ochre Group
Commercial Property Pty Ltd as trustee for Ochre Group Commercial
Property Unit Trust (together with their connected entities, Ochre
Health), support independent doctors to provide general practice
medical services in regional, rural and urban communities around
Australia.
Ochre Health currently operates 69 medical centres and urgent care
clinics located in the ACT, NSW, Queensland, Tasmania and Victoria,
but currently does not operate any medical centres or urgent care
clinics in Melbourne, Victoria. Ochre Health Group’s medical
recruitment business, Ochre Recruitment, also works with hospitals
and medical practices across Australia and NZ to source and place
locum and permanent doctors across a range of specialities.
Ochre Health is majority owned and controlled by Genesis Capital, an
Australian-based private equity firm that invests in businesses
operating in the healthcare sector.
AWC is a family medical practice with an all-female team of doctors
providing comprehensive general practice medical care to men,
women and children from its 3 clinics in the southeastern Melbourne
suburbs of Prahran and Malvern, Victoria.
Ochre Health and AWC supply general practice medical services in
Victoria, however Ochre Health does not operate any medical
centres or urgent care clinics within 80km of the AWC clinics.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no overlap in the supply of general practice medical
services in the local Prahran and Malvern areas or in the
greater Melbourne area
b. there are other clinics supplying general practice medical
services near Prahran and Malvern, Victoria and in the
greater Melbourne area.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
